---
title: "Announcing 'mini.input'"
author: Evgeni Chasnovski
date: 2026-06-04
categories: [announce]
description: "Get user input with fully customizable key and view handling"
---

![](2026-06-04-announce-mini-input/demo.mp4)

Hello, Neovim users!

Let's welcome the 'mini.nvim' module number 45: ['mini.input'](/mini.nvim/readmes/mini-input.md).

It's been a while since the latest new module (almost [six months](2025-12-07-announce-mini-cmdline.qmd)), so I am glad to finally make the release. This one was a long time in the works for various reasons:

- Somewhat important to polish the design (I'll try to upstream important part of it, stay tuned).

- Fighting mental battles of how much functionality to provide here. For better or worth, I ended up including quite a lot.

- MINI maintenance (even on top of the valuable help from the MINI team) takes priority.

- Working on upstream `vim.pack`.

On the bright side, 'mini.nvim' crossed a big milestone: it now has more than 5000 test cases! Not tested features are fragile features :)

------

Features of 'mini.input':

- Get user input with fully customizable key and view handling.

- Built-in configurable views as floating window, statusline/tabline/winbar, virtual line/text.

- Implementation is non-blocking but waits to return the input. It also works in any mode without requiring mode change. See [`:h MiniInput-lifecycle`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput-lifecycle).

- `vim.ui.input()` implementation. To adjust, use [`MiniInput.ui_input()`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput.ui_input) or save-restore `vim.ui.input` manually after calling [`MiniInput.setup()`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput.setup).


For more information see these parts of help:

- [`:h MiniInput.get()`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput.get)
- [`:h MiniInput.default_key()`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput.default_key)
- [`:h MiniInput-state`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput-state)
- [`:h MiniInput-examples`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-input.html#miniinput-examples)

------

It is also worth noting that 'mini.input' has special integrations across 'mini.nvim' modules that require synchronously getting user input. Like several surroundings of ['mini.surround'](/mini.nvim/readmes/mini-surround.md) (function, tag), interactive glob pattern input in [`:Pick grep_live`](/mini.nvim/doc/mini-pick.html#minipick.builtin.grep_live), and more.

------

Please, check it out and tell me what you think! Leave a comment in a dedicated [beta-testing issue](https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.nvim/issues/!!!!!!!!!!!!).

The 0.18 release is planned somewhat soon, so I'd be grateful if you'd take a look and help with beta-testing!

Thanks for reading! Let's hope that the next module will not take this long.
